description: Custom dictionary of allowed terms for the Curses! platform. All marketing, UI, and content copy should reference this dictionary for consistent terminology.

Curses!.usage: Always include the exclamation mark. Always italicize or bold in running text when referring to the product. Never lowercase.
Curses!.context: Product name. Used as a standalone noun: 'Curses! is a Daggerheart toolset.'

Man in Jumpsuit.usage: Full studio name. Do not abbreviate in legal/footer contexts.

Chronicles of Tidwell.usage: The actual-play show title. Italicize when possible.
Chronicles of Tidwell.context: Show name — the actual-play campaign streamed on Twitch.

Tales of Tidwell.usage: CMS content framing label for lore interstitials.
Tales of Tidwell.context: Used in the loading interstitial as a content heading.

Hope.usage: Always capitalized when referring to the Daggerheart resource.

Stress.usage: Always capitalized when referring to the Daggerheart resource.

HP.usage: Abbreviation for Hit Points. Always uppercase.

Hit Points.usage: Full form. Capitalize both words when used as a game term.

Domain.usage: Capitalized when referring to the Daggerheart character Domain.

Domain Card.usage: Two words, both capitalized. Refers to the cards associated with a Domain.

Loadout.usage: Capitalized when referring to a character's Domain Card Loadout.

Ancestry.usage: Capitalized. Refers to a character's species/heritage in Daggerheart.

Community.usage: Capitalized when referring to the Daggerheart character background option.

Class.usage: Capitalized when referring to a Daggerheart Class.

Subclass.usage: Capitalized. Refers to a specialization within a Class.

Adversary.usage: Capitalized. The SRD term for enemies/monsters in Daggerheart.

Hope Feature.usage: Capitalized. A character feature triggered by spending Hope.

Class Feature.usage: Capitalized. A feature granted by a character's Class.

Downtime.usage: Capitalized when referring to the Daggerheart Downtime phase.

Tier.usage: Capitalized when referring to Daggerheart advancement Tiers (Tier 1–4).

Companion.usage: Capitalized when referring to a character's Daggerheart Companion.

Fear.usage: Capitalized when referring to the Daggerheart resource/roll type.

with Hope / with Fear.usage: Daggerheart dual-die results. Always 'with Hope' or 'with Fear' — not 'on Hope.'

character sheet.usage: Lowercase in running text. Not a proper noun.

Encounter Designer.usage: Capitalized. Refers to the specific Curses! feature.

GM Command HUD.usage: Capitalized. Refers to the specific Curses! Game Master dashboard.

Homebrew Workshop.usage: Capitalized. Refers to the specific Curses! homebrew creation tool.

Dice Roller.usage: Capitalized when referring to the specific Curses! feature.

Session Log.usage: Capitalized when referring to the specific Curses! feature. Plural: Session Logs.

ping.usage: Lowercase as a verb ('ping an element'). The feature is the 'ping system' or 'GM ping.'

share link.usage: Lowercase. A permanent public URL to a character sheet.

SRD.usage: System Reference Document. No need to expand after first use in context where the audience understands it.

settingTerms.Tidwell: The world/continent of the Curses! campaign setting.
settingTerms.The Waste: Region name. Always 'The Waste' with capital T.
settingTerms.Forestdown: One word, capitalized. The corrupted temperate rainforest.
settingTerms.Varjalune Republic: Nation-state name. 'The Varjalune Republic' or just 'the Varjalune.'
settingTerms.Alipinn: Capital city of the Varjalune Republic.
settingTerms.Taliga: Second city of the Varjalune Republic.
settingTerms.Gatehouse: Border town, home of the Hygiane Order.
settingTerms.Hygiane Order: Military/religious order tasked with containing the Creep.
settingTerms.The Creep: The corrupting force within Forestdown. Always 'The Creep' with capital T.
settingTerms.Etherotaxia: Spirits and semi-corporeal beings in Forestdown.
settingTerms.The Spirit Ichida: Ancient ghost, embodiment of change. Always include 'The Spirit.'
settingTerms.Eraldama: A season in the Tidwell calendar.
settingTerms.Andam: Annual draft/tribute event. 'The Andam' or 'the 231st Andam.'
settingTerms.Storied Altar: The altar in Reveille through which peoples arrive.

formatting.em-dash: Use — (em dash) with no surrounding spaces for parenthetical breaks.
formatting.ampersand: Use '&' only in headings and stat strips. Use 'and' in body text.
